home.title: Helena Colaço | Artist
home.description: Helena Colaço is an artist based in Lisbon, Portugal. She creates art that explores the relationship between color, form, and emotion.
home.aboutDescription: With a life shaped by artistic exploration, from theatre to painting, photography, and poetry, she has built a rich and multidisciplinary creative path. Her work draws deeply from personal experience, performance, and visual storytelling, continually evolving between Lisbon and the south of Portugal.

drawing.title: Drawings
drawing.description: Drawing springs from the innermost expression of my being, whether they be simple doodles or elaborate works.

painting.title: Paintings
painting.description: Painting is freedom, it is gazing into the mirror of my soul, it is daring to make mistakes and move forward.

photography.title: Photographs
photography.description: A photograph is a moment frozen in the flow of life, illuminating its outline.

poem.title: Poems
poem.description: Poetry is the spontaneity of the soul channelling the collective memory of the sorrows and joys of being a human being.

watercolor.title: Watercolor Photographs
watercolor.description: Watercolour photographs is the moving image, moments that thaw and dissolve into memories of what once was, capturing the energy behind the photographic image.

newsletter.title: Stay in Touch
newsletter.description: Subscribe to our newsletter to stay updated with the latest creations, exhibitions and events.

contacts.title: Get in Touch
contacts.description: For commissions, collaborations or just to say hello.

meta.title: Biography - Helena Colaço
meta.description: Learn about Helena Colaço, a Portuguese multidisciplinary artist working across drawing, painting, photography and poetry.

content.name: Helena Colaço Salazar
content.p1: Her academic career has always been linked to the arts. She studied Architecture up to the third year of her degree at Lusíada University; her concept of how to inhabit the city led her to lose interest in this discipline. At the same time, she began her journey into the art of acting, studying and working as an actress for eleven years in Lisbon, exploring the facets of various characters and discovering a little more about herself and about human nature.
content.p2: In one of her most recent theatre performances, whilst developing her character, Kelly, from Gary Owen’s play *The Submerged World*, the words and message of the text began to take on such intensity that the best way to internalise the character was to express herself through drawing, compiling a series she called *Kelly’s Imaginary World*. This process marked another turning point in her career.
content.p3: In 2011, he began two courses. One in New Painting Techniques at the National Society of Fine Arts, taught by the painter Paiva Raposo, and one in Photography at the Portuguese Institute of Photography, exploring the images she sees and feels rather than being an image herself.
content.p4: Whilst still living in Lisbon, he undertook internships as a photojournalist at *Jornal Público* and *Jornal i*. He photographed architecture, live performances, vernissages, and photography and painting exhibitions.
content.p5: Another turning point in his life came when he felt suffocated by city life and decided he had to leave in order to survive; in 2014, he moved further south, to live by the beach, where he stayed until 2017. In his search for peace and a connection with nature, he embarked on a nomadic lifestyle for five years, travelling through Portugal, Spain, France, Israel and Central America.
content.p6: In 2023, he returned to the beachside, where he began work on his next solo painting exhibition, which was shown in 2025 at the Fábrica do Braço de Prata in Lisbon. Having participated in other group exhibitions.
content.p7: In 2026, he is developing his next artistic creation, using the photo-watercolour technique, in an exercise in fusing photography and painting, which he has titled: Diluted Memories

footer.headline: Through lines, colors, lenses, and words — a journey of self-expression, where each stroke, click, and verse unveils a new dimension of the soul.

cookieConsent.title: Cookies & analytics
cookieConsent.description: We use cookies for basic functionality and, with your permission, analytics to improve this website.

meta.title: Privacy Policy
meta.description: How we collect and use personal data and analytics.

controller.title: Who we are
controller.body: This website is operated by Helena Colaço. For any privacy-related questions, contact us at colaco.art@yahoo.com.

items.newsletter: Newsletter: name and email address (only when you submit the form).
items.analytics: Analytics: usage data (pages visited, interactions, approximate device/browser information) via PostHog, only after you accept analytics cookies.

items.newsletter: To send you updates if you subscribe to the newsletter.
items.analytics: To understand how the website is used and improve content and performance.

legalBasis.title: Legal basis
legalBasis.body: We process newsletter data and analytics based on your consent. You can withdraw consent at any time by rejecting analytics cookies or contacting us.

thirdParties.title: Third parties
thirdParties.body: We use PostHog as our analytics provider. Analytics are only enabled after you provide consent.

retention.title: How long we keep data
retention.body: We keep data for as long as necessary for the purposes described above, or until you withdraw consent.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body: You may request access, correction, or deletion of your personal data, and you can withdraw consent at any time. Contact us at colaco.art@yahoo.com.
